nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Abt  .

Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name from Apt, the Yiddish name of Opatów in Świętokrzyskie Voivodeship, Poland. The placename (in German, Yiddish, and Polish) is from a root meaning ‘abbot’, the place having been named for the local abbey. Compare   Abt  .

variants.name: Abt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Horst, Johann, Kurt.

German and Dutch: from Middle Low German or Middle Dutch abt ‘abbot’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a servant of an abbot or a nickname for someone thought to resemble an abbot in some way. In some instances it may have been a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with this word.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Abt  , a habitational name from Opatów in Poland.

variants.name: Apter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avrohom, Bronya, Eliezer, Naftali.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Apt (see   Apt  ).
English: habitational name from Aptor in Marldon, Devon, composed of Old English uppe ‘up, above’ + torr ‘rocky outcrop’.

nameMeanings.name: Ddewis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: locative name perhaps from Ewyas Harold or Ewyas Lacy (Herefs) with fused preposition, Anglo-Norman French de. Denias and Denyas are probably misreadings of -n- for -u, representing /w/ or /v/. Dewyas and Devyas developed to Dewis and Devis, and may have been confused with (2) and (3) from other origins.
2  English:  See  Devis  .
3  English:  See  Dews  .

variants.name: Devis
meanings: Mainly U.S.: transferred use of the surname, in origin a patronymic from the given name   Davy .  Use as a given name is often in honour of  Jefferson Davis  (  1808–89 ), President of the Confederate States during the Civil War.

variants.name: Jewess
meanings: 1 English: variant of Dewis with palatalization of Dew- to Jew-.
2  English:  see  Jewhurst  .

variants.name: Dewhurst
meanings: English: topographic name from Wilpshire in Lancashire, probably so named from Old English dēawig ‘dewy’ + hyrst ‘wooded hill’.

variants.name: Jewers
meanings: 1  English:  see  Gowers  .
2  English: alternatively perhaps a variant pronunciation of   Dewhurst  .
3  English: alternatively a variant of   Jewhurst  .

variants.name: Dew
meanings: Variant of   Dev .

variants.name: Dews
meanings: A Welsh equivalent (earlier Dewydd) of David , traditionally associated with the patron saint of Wales. See also Dai .

nameMeanings.name: Herod
meanings: Greek: An English female name, so called after the celebrated Greek name, which may translate 'One Raised or Elevated,' to lift

en.lifeSummary: Cynwrig Ddewis Herod ap Cowryd was born about 1230, in Llanfair Dyffryn Clwyd, Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom as the son of Cowryd ap Perfarch. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter.
